name: Bent Knee Lying Twist on Stability Ball
aliases: Bent knee Lying  twist (On Stability Ball)
shortDescription: A rotational stretch on a stability ball that targets the obliques and hip flexors.
instructions: Lie on your back with your calves resting on a stability ball, arms extended to sides. With knees bent, gently rotate your knees side to side, engaging your core to control the movement.
steps: Lie on your back with calves on a stability ball.
Extend arms out at your sides for support.
Bend knees at 90 degrees over the ball.
Slowly drop knees to one side, keeping shoulders flat.
Return to center and repeat on the other side.
breathing: Exhale as you twist, inhale returning to center.
